2024-04-26.thesis: ADVANCE Act bipartisan bill streamlines NRC licensing for SMR developers
2024-04-26.exit_case: Bill not yet signed — ADVANCE Act still pending in Congress as of entry week; no resolution.
2024-04-26.assessment: First week on this event. The ADVANCE Act is a bipartisan bill advancing through Congress to streamline NRC licensing and reduce fees for advanced nuclear/SMR developers — a specific, datable legislative catalyst that has not yet resolved. NuScale Power (SMR) is the cleanest pure-play: the only US SMR developer with an NRC-approved design, giving it the most direct and immediate leverage to the bill's passage. AI-driven data-center power demand is a tailwind amplifying interest but is not the catalyst itself. Coverage is early-stage (niche energy/carbon press, not front-page mainstream), conviction is solid. Basket should include SMR as anchor and OKLO as a peer SMR-licensing beneficiary. Silence between floor votes = hold; bill signed or killed = exit.
2024-04-26.exit_advice: Hold. The ADVANCE Act has not yet been signed into law. The catalyst is a discrete, datable legislative event — exit only on presidential signature (resolution) or a definitive kill vote (reversal). Do not exit on silence between floor votes.

2024-06-21.thesis: ADVANCE Act bipartisan bill streamlines NRC licensing for SMR developers
2024-06-21.exit_case: ADVANCE Act cleared Senate June 18 and is on the President's desk — the legislative catalyst has effectively resolved.
2024-06-21.assessment: After seven consecutive weeks of silence decay compressing conviction from 7 all the way to 1, the catalyst has finally fired: the Senate passed the ADVANCE Act on June 18, 2024, sending the nuclear-licensing streamlining bill to the President's desk. This is the specific, datable legislative event this position was entered on. Congressional passage is the operative resolution milestone — presidential signature of a bipartisan, Senate-passed bill is a formality, not a new catalyst. The information is now public and mainstream. Per the rules, catalyst_resolved=true forces thesis_live=false even if SMR and peers continue to move on signing news. The entire thesis arc (entry → pending bill → Senate passage → President's desk) is complete. No further concrete pending legislative milestone remains. Exit in full.
2024-06-21.exit_advice: EXIT SMR. The ADVANCE Act passed the Senate on June 18 and is headed to the President for signature — a bipartisan bill at this stage is a near-certain signing formality. The specific legislative catalyst (Congressional passage of NRC-licensing overhaul) is now fully public and priced. The early-entry edge — owning before floor votes and Senate passage — has been completely spent. Close the position regardless of any residual rally on the presidential signing itself; that would be a backward-looking, already-discounted event, not an early-mover opportunity.
